response.justification: Strong candidate already working as Staff Software Engineer at an AI-native company with direct agentic infrastructure and MCP experience. Excellent distributed systems background (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby) and strong TypeScript/Node.js expertise. However, Python is explicitly a must-have technical skill and is completely absent from the resume; operational ownership and on-call experience are not mentioned. These gaps require clarification but don't disqualify given the current role match and technical depth.
strengths: Currently Staff Software Engineer at AI-native company (re:cinq) working on agent infrastructure and agentic workflows
Proven high-throughput distributed systems expertise: micro-services handling millions of HTTP/second with low-latency async I/O and caching
Strong TypeScript/Node.js foundation across multiple senior roles with demonstrated ownership mindset (founded GISCollective, took over iOS/tvOS apps when developer left)
concerns: Python is a stated must-have technical skill but is completely absent from resume despite extensive language list
No explicit mention of operational ownership, monitoring, incident response, or on-call rotation experience
Identity inconsistencies: resume name (AISHA OKONKWO) does not match online profiles (szabobogdan GitHub/GitLab/LinkedIn/website), raising verification concerns

key_factors.factor: Current AI-native environment at exact seniority level
